last_item: Dashboard — removed the always-blank Postgres 'Version' row (backend never populates it; rendered a permanent em-dash that read as missing data).
last_result: SHIPPED. Review panel 4 SHIP, zero blockers (UX heuristics + Sam + Priya + restraint critic). Honest-state deletion: ~4 lines removed from the view; test fixture's misleading version value dropped + assertion added that the row no longer renders. Gates green: typecheck, 134 tests, build, go build (outside sandbox). Both personas flagged that a REAL PG version is worth surfacing later on a details/settings page — captured as a future idea, not backlogged. Next top item: Alerts — warn when rules exist but no notification channel is enabled (silent-failure, high/S).
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn — rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres — minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
